claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 10 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start: **How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my two children. 

I have Alex, who is 10 years old, and Blake, who is 2 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to know you have Alex (10 years old) and Blake (2 years old).

Now, let me ask: **Do both Alex and Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

Next question: **Are you responsible for both children** – meaning you pay for things like their food, clothes, and day-to-day care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for both of them. I pay for their food, clothes, and everything they need day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask about your own circumstances: **Are you aged 16 or over?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm over 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I have a couple more questions about residency and immigration status.

**Do you live in the UK?** (This includes England, Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. That's an important detail.

To understand your situation better: **Are you living abroad temporarily, or is this a permanent arrangement?** 

Also, **are you a Crown servant posted overseas (for example, a diplomat or member of the armed forces), or are you the family member of a Crown servant posted overseas?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I'm not a diplomat or in the armed forces, and neither is my partner. I'm just living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Based on what you've told me, I need to ask one more clarifying question.

The rules state that you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in "certain countries." 

**Which country are you currently living in?** This will help me determine if there are any special arrangements that might apply to your situation.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living in Spain at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know you're in Spain. Since Spain is an EU country, I need to ask a few more questions about your immigration status and circumstances.

**Do you have either settled status or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Given that you're living abroad in Spain, you don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and you're not a Crown servant, I have one more question to make sure I've covered all possibilities:

**Are you currently paying UK National Insurance contributions, or do you have any ongoing connection to the UK such as working for a UK employer?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not paying UK National Insurance contributions, and I don't work for a UK employer. I don't have any ongoing connection to the UK like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me, I'm afraid the situation is as follows:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm sorry to say that **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake**.

Here's a summary of my assessment:

### Alex (age 10) – **Not Eligible**
While Alex is under 16 and you are responsible for them, the core requirement for Child Benefit is that you must live in the UK. Since you're living in Spain and don't qualify under any of the exceptions (Crown servant, settled status, or pre-settled status with qualifying conditions), you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

### Blake (age 2) – **Not Eligible**
The same reasoning applies to Blake. Although Blake is under 16 and you are responsible for them, your residence outside the UK means you don't meet the eligibility criteria.

---

**If your circumstances change** – for example, if you move back to the UK – you would likely become eligible to claim Child Benefit for both children, as they are both under 16 and you are responsible for them.

You may also wish to explore whether Spain offers any equivalent family benefits for residents there.

I hope this information is helpful, and I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. If you have any further questions, please feel free to ask.

reasonings: To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK and be responsible for a child under 16. While Alex is under 16 and you are responsible for them, you do not live in the UK. The exceptions for living abroad include: being a Crown servant posted overseas (which you are not), having settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (which you don't have), or having pre-settled status with certain qualifying conditions (which you don't have). Since you live in Spain, are not paying UK National Insurance, have no connection to a UK employer, and don't fall into any of the special categories that allow claiming Child Benefit while abroad, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK and be responsible for a child under 16. While Blake is under 16 and you are responsible for them, you do not live in the UK. The exceptions for living abroad include: being a Crown servant posted overseas (which you are not), having settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (which you don't have), or having pre-settled status with certain qualifying conditions (which you don't have). Since you live in Spain, are not paying UK National Insurance, have no connection to a UK employer, and don't fall into any of the special categories that allow claiming Child Benefit while abroad, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for two children under 16 (Alex, age 10, and Blake, age 2) who live with them. However, the claimant does not live in the UK – they are currently residing in Spain. They are not a Crown servant, do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, are not paying UK National Insurance contributions, and have no ongoing connection to the UK such as working for a UK employer. The fundamental requirement for Child Benefit is that you normally qualify if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. Since the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirement and does not fall into any of the special categories that allow claiming while living abroad, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.
